hero.title: Where will you study abroad?
hero.subtitle: We work with Francophone African students to secure admission at top institutions across seven countries.
hero.quote: He who follows others can only go as far as where they are going, but he who creates his own path can go as far as he wants to.

video.eyebrow: Take a look inside one of our partner schools
video.title: A first look at BCIT, Burnaby, BC
video.caption: BCIT — British Columbia Institute of Technology, a real Masomo Now partner school.

canada.tagline: From coast to coast — technical institutes to universities
canada.desc: Canada remains the single most requested destination for our students. Beyond the FMC Student Pilot's francophone pathway, we also place English-track students directly with technical institutes, colleges, and universities across British Columbia, Alberta, Saskatchewan, and Ontario — from Metro Vancouver's tech corridor to smaller regional colleges with a lower cost of living.
pros: Post-Graduation Work Permit — up to 3 years
A recognised pathway toward permanent residence
TRU's guaranteed tuition model locks in your rate for your whole program
Lower cost of living at regional colleges (Northern Lights, North Island)

united-states.tagline: A community-college pathway into the Seattle tech corridor
united-states.desc: Our two Washington State partners sit minutes from downtown Seattle — home to Amazon, Microsoft, and one of the world's busiest tech job markets. Both offer accredited two-year degrees and certificates, with clear transfer pathways into four-year bachelor's programs, at a fraction of the cost of direct university enrollment.
pros: Direct pathway into the Seattle tech corridor
Clear transfer pathways to bachelor's degrees
No TOEFL/IELTS required at LWTech (free placement test)

ireland.tagline: English-speaking gateway to the European Union
ireland.desc: Ireland is one of the only English-speaking countries in the EU, making it a strategic choice for African students seeking a globally recognised degree with access to European job markets. Dublin is home to the European headquarters of Google, Meta, and Apple.

germany.tagline: Europe's largest economy — English programs available
germany.desc: Germany is Europe's strongest economy and a top destination for international students. Our three partner institutions offer fully English-taught business and management programs, making Germany accessible without needing to learn German first.

poland.desc: Poland offers EU-recognised degrees at significantly lower tuition fees and living costs than Western Europe. Vistula University in Warsaw is one of Poland's most internationally active institutions, with a strong track record of enrolling students from Africa.
pros: Among the most affordable EU tuitions
Lower cost of living vs. Western Europe

australia.tagline: Globally ranked engineering education with strong post-study work rights
australia.desc: Australia is home to globally ranked universities and a thriving engineering and technology job market. Our partner institution in Perth offers industry-focused, practical engineering education with strong ties to employers.
pros: Temporary Graduate visa (subclass 485) pathway after graduation
Strong engineering and resources-sector job market in Western Australia
Growing community of African students in Australia

china.tagline: Access to 600+ universities through one national platform
factValues: No universal permit — requires an employer-sponsored work visa (Z visa)
china.desc: China offers one of the world's largest and most diverse higher education systems. Through CUCAS (China's University and College Admission System), students can apply to over 600 colleges and universities nationwide from a single platform, with access to full scholarships including the Chinese Government Scholarship and university-specific awards.
pros: Single application platform for 600+ colleges and universities
Access to full scholarships (Chinese Government Scholarship and university awards)
English-taught program options at many institutions
Lower tuition and living costs than many Western destinations
Growing African student community, supported by our Nanjing office

comingSoon.title: Expanding to more regions
comingSoon.photoCredits: Campus photography courtesy of Wikimedia Commons contributors (CC BY-SA / public domain); Fanshawe College and Northern Lights College photos are ELIMU's own event photography.

compare.title: Compare Destinations
compare.disclaimer: Representative ranges, not guarantees — actual costs vary by institution and city. Living costs reflect each country's official minimum funds requirement, except Poland, where the legal minimum is unrealistically low and a realistic city living-cost estimate is shown instead.

united-states.work: On-campus only; CPT/OPT for practical training

poland.work: No strict hour cap for full-time students

australia.work: 48 hrs/fortnight during term, unlimited during breaks

china.work: Limited — generally not permitted without special permission
china.gradVisa: No universal permit — requires a Z (work) visa

cta.title: Not sure which country is right for you?
cta.body: Answer 6 quick questions and we'll match you to a real pathway based on your goals, budget, and language preference.
